So many choices have been laid before me. I must turn down nearly all, focus on 3. do 12 hour days. Create masterpiece.
It takes everything I have within me. It is crushing, but it will be worth it.
I finally have enough to draw on to do it properly, perfectly. One time.
It began a half a year ago, 3 hours a day. Now 12 hours. Time.
I have one thing, and one thing only, to say. It has taken all this time to get to it. And now it nears completion. 3 in 1.
Nothing easy is good, nothing good easy. Quantity is for butchers. One thing.That's all a man can say. One thing.
Reclusive, Monastic, Alone, Alone, Alone. It comes slowly but surely. It comes in ocean-deep silences. It is a sun to stare at.
For twenty years, i believed I would not have the time to find it. It was there, but out of my grasp.
My eyes were aging and the truth was getting blurry.
And then I stood stock-still and let it tell me. Tell me what to say. One thing. But one in three.
And so I have to show away all the opportunities laying at my feet. It would be profane.
Pride goeth before the fall, and I must hit the floor hard with my knees nightly, should I get vain.
Falling is out of the question. Unacceptable.
I have planned it out for so long. And then had to wait. and wait. and wait.
But when it hit me on that dirt road, it hit me hard in the chest.
I'd been shooting a tree with a submachine gun, and it never fell.
But i did. I fell in a road of dirt and sweat and blood and slept deep through the night.
I've seen a hundred poems lovelier than a tree. Fact, Jack.
12 hours every single day. Nothing to do but do it. And I have to create diamond clarity from blurry images I can barely see.
But where my eyes are weak, my mind takes over. It is coming very soon.
MIND THE GAP.
